zh.lifeSummary: 當George Caldwell於大約1582年出生在英國爾格拉本哈爾時，他的父親John Caldwell35歲，母親Margaret Hylde35歲。他於1607年2月1日與Elizabeth Fleetwood在英國爾格拉本哈爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1624年2月3日在英國藍卡爾頓享年43歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当George Caldwell于大约1582年出生在英国爾格拉本哈爾时，他的父亲John Caldwell35岁，母亲Margaret Hylde35岁。他于1607年2月1日与Elizabeth Fleetwood在英国爾格拉本哈爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1624年2月3日在英国藍卡爾頓享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: Caldwell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish: habitational name from any of several places in England and Scotland, variously spelled, that are named with Old English ceald ‘cold’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’. Caldwell in North Yorkshire is one major source of the surname; Caldwell in Renfrewshire in Scotland another. Possibly also from Caldwell (Warwickshire), Caldwall (Worcestershire), Cauldwell (Bedfordshire, Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire), Caudle Green (Gloucestershire), Caudle Ditch or Cawdle Fen (Cambridgeshire), Chadwell (Essex, Hertfordshire, Leicestershire, Wiltshire), Chardwell (Essex), or Chardle Ditch (Cambridgeshire, early recorded as Kadewelle).

Irish: when not the English surname, this is an Anglicized form of Ó Fuarghuis or Ó hUarghusa ‘descendant of (F)uarghus’, a personal name whose literal sense ‘cold’ + ‘choice’ was reinterpreted as coming from fuaruisce ‘cold water’.

History: Several Caldwells emigrated from Scotland to America by way of Ireland in the 18th century. James Caldwell (1734–81), a son of settler John Caldwell, was born in Charlotte County, VA, and was a militant clergyman during the revolutionary war. Andrew Caldwell, a Scottish farmer, emigrated to North America in 1718 and started a family in Lancaster County, PA. His son David was a Presbyterian clergyman and well-known revolutionary war patriot.

variants.name: Cadle
meanings: English (Gloucestershire) and Irish, both of Norman origin: nickname from Old French cadel ‘leader’.
English: variant of   Caldwell   and of   Caudill  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German   Kadel  .

variants.name: Cardwell
meanings: English and Scottish: in northern England, the West Midlands, and southwest Scotland, this surname is a variant of Caldwell . In southwestern England it is probably a variant of Cardall, a habitational name from Cardwell in Milton Abbot (Devon). See also Cardell .

variants.name: Cadwell
meanings: (Cornwall and Devon): habitational name from one of two Devon places, either Cadewell (House) in Cockington or Caddywell in Great Torrington.

variants.name: Caudill
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English caudel, cadel, caudell(e) ‘hot fortified drink, esp. one containing spiced wine or ale’, also a thin gruel (Anglo-Norman French caudel, medieval Latin caldellum). Alternatively, a variant of Caldwell . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Cordwell
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of Caldwell . Alternatively, in Derbyshire a habitational name from Cordwell in Holmsfield, Derbyshire, named with Old English cauld ‘cold’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When George Caldwell was born about 1582, in Grappenhall, Cheshire, England, his father, John Caldwell, was 35 and his mother, Margaret Hylde, was 35. He married Elizabeth Fleetwood on 1 February 1607, in Grappenhall, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 3 February 1624, in Huyton, Lancashire, England, at the age of 43.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда George Caldwell родился около 1582 г. в местности граппенолл, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Caldwell было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Hylde, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Fleetwood 1 февраля 1607 г. в местности граппенолл, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 3 февраля 1624 г. в местности хуитон, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 43 лет.
